status_notes: Initial research from Azure VM documentation, 2026-04-04. Needs cross-reference pass and human review with live Azure environment.
notes: Virtual machines for Azure. Security includes disk encryption, Trusted Launch, Confidential VMs, managed identity, update management.

match_nodes.description: Encrypt OS and data disks at rest

data.description: Multiple encryption options for VM disks. Azure Disk Encryption uses BitLocker (Windows) or DM-Crypt (Linux) for volume-level encryption. Server-side encryption (SSE) encrypts data at the storage layer. Encryption at host encrypts data on the VM host itself.

options.name: Server-side encryption with platform-managed keys
options.description: Default. Microsoft manages the encryption keys. AES-256.

options.name: Server-side encryption with customer-managed keys
options.description: You manage keys in Azure Key Vault. Full key lifecycle control.

options.name: Azure Disk Encryption (ADE)
options.description: Volume-level encryption using BitLocker (Windows) or DM-Crypt (Linux). Keys stored in Key Vault.

options.name: Encryption at host
options.description: Encrypts temp disk, OS/data disk caches on the VM host. End-to-end encryption.

default.note: All managed disks are encrypted at rest with platform-managed keys by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use customer-managed keys for sensitive workloads. Enable encryption at host for end-to-end encryption including temp disks and caches.

match_nodes.description: Secure boot + vTPM protecting against bootkits and rootkits

data.description: Trusted launch provides foundational security for Gen2 VMs. Secure boot ensures only signed bootloaders and kernels execute. vTPM provides a hardware-backed virtual Trusted Platform Module for measured boot and key storage. Integrity monitoring detects boot chain tampering.

options.description: Validates bootloader and kernel signatures. Blocks unsigned or tampered boot code.
Virtual TPM 2.0 for measured boot, BitLocker, key storage. Hardware-backed.
Boot integrity attestation. Alerts on boot chain changes via Microsoft Defender for Cloud.

default.note: Available for Gen2 VMs. Enabled by default for new VMs in many regions.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable Trusted Launch for all production VMs. Use Gen2 VM images that support Trusted Launch.

configure.cli: az vm create --resource-group {rg} --name {vm} --security-type TrustedLaunch --enable-secure-boot true --enable-vtpm true ...

match_nodes.description: Hardware-based memory encryption using AMD SEV-SNP or Intel TDX

data.description: Confidential VMs encrypt memory in use with a hardware-managed key. The hypervisor and host OS cannot read the VM's memory. Uses AMD SEV-SNP or Intel TDX. Provides cryptographic attestation of the VM's trusted execution environment.

options.name: AMD SEV-SNP
options.description: AMD Secure Encrypted Virtualization with Secure Nested Paging. DCasv5/ECasv5 series.

options.name: Intel TDX
options.description: Intel Trust Domain Extensions. DCesv5/ECesv5 series.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use for highly sensitive workloads requiring protection from cloud operator access. Combine with confidential OS disk encryption for full stack protection.

match_nodes.description: Microsoft Entra ID identity for VMs — eliminates credential management

data.description: Provides an automatically managed identity in Microsoft Entra ID for the VM. Applications running on the VM can use this identity to obtain tokens for Azure resources without storing credentials.

options.name: System-assigned
options.description: Identity tied to the VM lifecycle. Created and deleted with the VM.

options.name: User-assigned
options.description: Standalone identity resource. Can be assigned to multiple VMs. Independent lifecycle.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use managed identity instead of stored credentials. Use user-assigned for shared identities across multiple VMs. Apply least-privilege RBAC to the identity.

match_nodes.description: Network Security Group association on NIC or subnet

data.description: Network Security Groups filter network traffic to and from Azure resources. Can be associated with a VM's network interface (NIC) or the subnet. Rules are stateful, evaluated by priority (lower number = higher priority).
data.vendor_recommendation: Apply NSGs at both subnet and NIC levels for defense in depth. Use application security groups (ASGs) for dynamic grouping. Avoid overly permissive rules.

audit.cli_subnet: az network vnet subnet show --vnet-name {vnet} --name {subnet} --resource-group {rg} --query networkSecurityGroup

data.description: Automated OS patching for Azure VMs. Azure Update Manager provides a unified view of update compliance. Automatic guest patching downloads and installs patches automatically. Supports both Windows and Linux.

options.description: Unified patch management. Assess, schedule, and deploy updates. Replaces legacy Update Management.
Azure-orchestrated patching. Downloads and applies patches during maintenance windows.
Apply security patches without reboot (Windows Server Azure Edition only).

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable Azure Update Manager for all VMs. Use automatic guest patching for non-critical workloads. Schedule maintenance windows for production.

match_nodes.description: Defender for Cloud JIT VM access — time-limited port opening

data.description: Just-in-time (JIT) VM access reduces exposure by locking down inbound traffic to management ports (SSH/RDP). When access is needed, a user requests it through Defender for Cloud, which temporarily opens the port for a specified duration and source IP.
data.vendor_recommendation: Enable JIT access for all VMs with management ports. Set maximum request duration. Require justification for access requests.

audit.console: Defender for Cloud → Workload protections → Just-in-time VM access

configure.console: Defender for Cloud → Just-in-time VM access → Configure on a VM
configure.note: Requires Microsoft Defender for Servers Plan 2.

match_nodes.description: Managed bastion host for RDP/SSH without public IP

data.description: Fully managed PaaS service providing secure RDP/SSH connectivity to VMs directly through the Azure portal over TLS. VMs do not need public IP addresses, agent, or special client software.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use Azure Bastion instead of public IPs for administrative access. Combine with JIT access for defense in depth.

match_nodes.description: Serial console and screenshot for troubleshooting — may contain sensitive output

data.description: Captures serial log output and screenshots of the VM during boot. Useful for diagnosing boot failures. Serial console provides interactive text-based access. Boot diagnostic data may contain sensitive information (error messages with paths, usernames, configuration details).
data.vendor_recommendation: Enable for troubleshooting but be aware that serial output may contain sensitive information. Use managed storage account (default) to avoid managing storage access separately.

match_nodes.description: Encrypt temp disk, OS/data disk caches on the VM host

data.description: Encryption at host ensures that data stored on the VM host is encrypted at rest. Covers temp disks, OS disk caches, and data disk caches. Complements server-side encryption which covers the managed disk storage. Together they provide end-to-end encryption.

default.note: Not enabled by default. Must be explicitly enabled. Requires subscription-level feature registration.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable encryption at host for VMs handling sensitive data to ensure temp disks and caches are encrypted.
